conversations.content: Let's do an Azeroth world magic exercise to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the previously mentioned Azeroth world magic rules: You are a Shaman. During the stand-still phase of a raid, you cast 'Molten Mantle' for the melee DPS. What will the teammates standing on it gain? What will happen to the enemies who attack the teammates in melee?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Previously mentioned Azeroth world magic rules, the effects on allies and melee-attacking enemies of a Shaman casting 'Molten Mantle'</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:104] Time: 2025-07-26-09:23, Content: The user taught the casting rules of the Azeroth elemental magic 'Molten Mantle'. The prerequisites are the Shaman class and The Earthen Ring reputation Exalted; the core effect is summoning an area of lava. Allies standing on it gain a shield equal to 20% of their maximum health, and melee attackers are set on fire, lasting for 12 seconds; applicable to protection during stand-still DPS phases in raids and damage reduction for melee DPS. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There is 1 related entry mentioned in the memory. [mem-id:104] indicates that 'Molten Mantle' grants allies standing on it a shield equal to 20% of their maximum health and sets melee attackers on fire. Therefore, the teammates gain a shield, and the enemies will catch fire.
conversations.content: According to the rules, the melee DPS will gain a shield equal to 20% of their maximum health, and the enemies who attack them in melee will catch fire, lasting for 12 seconds.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
